_risk_level_doc: Change is confined to three files: two command/skill definitions and one policy JSON. No security boundary is crossed; changes are additive (new allowed prefix, no removal of denials). Hook logic is unchanged.

requirement.original: Establish docs/codex/<task_id>/<role>.txt as the canonical Codex output path; update tool-policy.v1.json to grant write access to that prefix for relevant roles; update the codex Skill definition so the default output target reflects that path.
requirement.clarified: Add */docs/codex/ to allowed_write_path_prefixes for 11 Codex-consultation roles (dev excluded — wildcard *); update commands/codex.md and /root/.agents/skills/codex/SKILL.md Step 2 with path-safety-validated canonical path logic; update all 6 tee sites in both files to use hook-resolvable forms only; demote /var/tmp/codex-outputs/ to fallback.
requirement.what: Grant write access to docs/codex/ prefix for 11 roles; establish docs/codex/<task_id>/<role>.txt as canonical default in both Skill files; ensure all tee sites use hook-resolvable path forms
requirement.why: bash_write_targets.py::_resolve_path() cannot expand $CODEX_OUT — hook sees the literal token and blocks writes even to allowed paths. Fixing requires: (a) literal/CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR-prefixed tee targets, AND (b) adding docs/codex/ as allowed prefix

included: Adding */docs/codex/ to allowed_write_path_prefixes for ba, qa, pm, architect, product-owner, user, ui-specialist, spec, prompt-inspector, cleanliness-inspector, style-inspector (11 roles)
Updating commands/codex.md Step 2: path-safety guard + canonical path branch + fallback
Updating all 6 tee sites in both files to hook-resolvable forms
excluded: Modifying any hook file or lib/ Python modules
Creating docs/codex/ directory in git (runtime mkdir -p handles it)

Adding docs/codex/ to dev (wildcard * already covers it), timeline, transportation, test-writer, test-executor, test-validator, cleaner, git-edge-case-analyst, rule-inspector, changelog-analyst, push-analyst, merge-analyst, pull-analyst, general-purpose

success_criteria: Exactly 11 roles (set-verified via JSON) have */docs/codex/ in allowed_write_path_prefixes; dev unchanged with wildcard *
commands/codex.md Step 2 generates $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex/<task_id>/<role>.txt (not flat path) with path-safety guard
All six tee sites in both files use hook-resolvable path forms — no standalone $VAR tee target
SKILL.md Step 2 block matches commands/codex.md identically
constraints: Do not modify hook files or lib/ Python modules
Policy path prefix must be */docs/codex/ (glob form per policy _note fnmatch semantics)
Tee targets must use $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex/$CODEX_OUT_TASK_ID/$CODEX_OUT_ROLE.txt or literal fallback — no standalone opaque shell variable
Path-safety guard ^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$ must be present for both env var components

path_dependent_shared_infra: hooks/lib/bash_write_targets.py (read only — _resolve_path behavior governs tee form requirement)

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Subagents invoking codex Skill via tee are blocked by pretool-tool-policy.py even when the underlying path would be allowed
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: bash_write_targets.py::_resolve_path() (lines 152-170) expands only $HOME, ~, $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR. A tee with '$CODEX_OUT' yields the literal token '$CODEX_OUT' to the policy checker, which matches no allowed prefix. Additionally, docs/codex/ is not yet present in any role's allowed_write_path_prefixes.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — missing feature, not a regression
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: /var/tmp/codex-outputs/ was chosen for disk-backed storage to avoid tmpfs ENOSPC; no structured per-task-per-role path or hook-resolvable tee form was designed at that time
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Codex output is ephemeral and not discoverable by task/role; tee is blocked at the hook before the policy can even match the /var/tmp/ prefix because the token is the literal string $CODEX_OUT
root_cause_analysis.timeline: 2026-05-21 — first attempt to address this gap; prior ticket fa9f07 (same cycle) missed SKILL.md and 2 roles
observations: Observed: Codex verified via extract_bash_write_paths('codex exec x | tee "$CODEX_OUT"') → ['$CODEX_OUT'] — hook sees literal token, not expanded path
Observed: _resolve_path() source at hooks/lib/bash_write_targets.py:152-170 shows only $HOME/~/$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR are expanded
Inferred: policy file read confirms 0 roles have */docs/codex/ in allowed_write_path_prefixes
Inferred: grep confirms 12 agents invoke Skill(skill="codex"); dev has wildcard *, leaving 11 needing */docs/codex/ added
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: complete — bash_write_targets.py behavior verified by source read and confirmed by Codex runtime test
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Source read of bash_write_targets.py:152-170 confirms _resolve_path only expands 3 specific tokens; Codex ran extract_bash_write_paths in the repo and confirmed the literal $CODEX_OUT token result

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: not applicable — missing feature gap, not a regression

context.codebase_state: master branch; policies/tool-policy.v1.json at policy_version 3; commands/codex.md and SKILL.md at current state with /var/tmp/codex-outputs/ as sole output path and tee "$CODEX_OUT" at all 6 tee sites
context.recent_commits: 1a1d058 feat(dev-cycle): Cycle 2 remediation; 50f2ca2 fix(agents): tighten style-inspector; 6537d7d fix(hooks,commit): close-gate subproject path-walk

development_approach.strategy: Three-file additive change: (1) insert */docs/codex/ into 11 role entries in policy JSON, increment policy_version, update _note; (2) replace Step 2 in commands/codex.md with path-resolution block + update all 3 tee sites to hook-resolvable forms; (3) apply identical changes to /root/.agents/skills/codex/SKILL.md.
development_approach.validation_approach: JSON-semantic role set check (Python set comparison); policy_version field check; codex.md and SKILL.md text inspection for canonical path shape and no opaque-variable tee targets; SKILL.md/codex.md block comparison

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh reasoning) raised 5 findings and 1 observation. CF-1: AC1 count-based check can be satisfied with wrong role set — needs set-based verification. CF-2: AC3 too weak — 'contains docs/codex/' passes flat non-structured paths. CF-3: path-safety check for dynamic env vars prevents traversal (already in draft but needed AC wording). CF-4: AC4 negative check too narrow — must cover all standalone-variable tee forms not just $CODEX_OUT. CF-5: AC4/AC5 ambiguous — must explicitly say 'all six tee sites across both files'. CF-6 (OBSERVATION_ONLY): 11-role list and dev exclusion confirmed correct.

findings.issue: AC1 can pass with wrong roles — jq count of 11 satisfied by duplicates or wrong role. Also does not verify dev wildcard unchanged.
findings.proposed_fix: Make AC1 set-based: exact role set must equal {ba, qa, pm, architect, product-owner, user, ui-specialist, spec, prompt-inspector, cleanliness-inspector, style-inspector}; dev must retain wildcard *.
findings.rationale: AC1 now uses Python set comparison against the exact 11-role set; dev wildcard check added.

findings.issue: AC3 'contains docs/codex/' could pass with flat path like docs/codex/codex-output-$$.txt — not the required <task_id>/<role>.txt structure.
findings.proposed_fix: AC3 must require path shape $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex/<task_id>/<role>.txt with no literal placeholders.
findings.rationale: AC3 now explicitly requires the two-level path structure and the path-safety guard.

findings.issue: Path-component safety check for dynamic task_id/role needed — unsafe values could escape docs/codex/ prefix while hook sees a valid prefix.
findings.proposed_fix: Add must-have and AC: canonical branch only when task_id and role match ^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$; otherwise fallback.
findings.rationale: M2 and AC3 now both require the ^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$ guard.

findings.issue: AC4 negative check too narrow — bans only tee "$CODEX_OUT" but not tee "${CODEX_OUT}", tee "$OUT", or other standalone-variable forms.
findings.proposed_fix: Prohibit any tee target that is a standalone opaque shell variable; require each target to include a hook-visible allowed prefix directly.
findings.rationale: AC4 now explicitly prohibits all standalone-variable forms including ${CODEX_OUT}, $OUT, etc.

findings.issue: AC4/AC5 ambiguous — 'all three tee sites' could mean only one file. Both files have three each = six total.
findings.proposed_fix: State 'all six tee sites: three in commands/codex.md and three in SKILL.md'.
findings.rationale: AC4 and AC5 now both explicitly reference all six tee sites across both files.

findings.issue: 11-role update list is directionally correct; spec and user inclusion supported by agents/*.md grep evidence; dev exclusion correct per wildcard *.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied all 5 blocker/major findings from Codex CF-1 through CF-5. Self-review covered: (1) role set accuracy, (2) path shape specificity, (3) path-safety guard, (4) all opaque-var tee forms, (5) six-tee-site coverage across both files.
